evals.name: architecture opportunities context first
evals.prompt: A user asks: find refactoring opportunities to make this repo more testable and AI-navigable. Describe the architecture review workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response reads CONTEXT/ADRs, uses module/interface/seam/depth vocabulary, finds deepening opportunities not cosmetic cleanup, applies deletion test and seam rule, and explains testability benefits.
assertions: The output says to read CONTEXT.md, CONTEXT-MAP.md, or docs/adr before naming architecture findings.
The output uses architecture vocabulary such as module, interface, seam, adapter, depth, leverage, or locality.
The output distinguishes architecture deepening from cosmetic or line-level cleanup.
The output applies the deletion test to identify shallow modules or pass-through abstractions.
The output applies seam discipline such as needing real variation before adding interfaces.
The output explains how proposed changes improve testability or AI navigation.

evals.name: boundary not line cleanup
evals.prompt: A user asks this architecture skill to rename variables, fix formatting, and clean up comments in one file. Describe how to respond.
evals.expected_output: The response identifies this as line-level cleanup rather than architecture improvement, routes to reviewing-code/refactoring-code or normal edit workflow, and avoids inventing architecture findings.
assertions: The output says the request is not an architecture-deepening task.
The output routes the work to line-level review, refactoring-code, or normal editing rather than architecture review.
The output avoids using architecture vocabulary to justify cosmetic changes.
The output asks for confirmation if the user actually wants deeper architecture analysis.
The output keeps scope limited to the requested cleanup if proceeding.
